summary: I am originally from Thailand, where I lived in the Mae La Oon camp. My family moved there seeking a better future and education after leaving Klo Pa. Thailand became my home for many years, especially during my school days at Yaung Ni Oo High School.

Mae La Oon camp in Thailand is where my family settled after leaving Klo Pa. I attended Yaung Ni Oo High School there, which had to relocate to Mae La Oon after floods affected its original location. Life in Mae La Oon was challenging but it became the place where I grew up and learned many important lessons.

Now I live in St. Paul, Minnesota in the United States. My family moved here in October 2014, although I had not finished school yet. Moving to St. Paul was a big change for me, with many new experiences and challenges, but I am grateful to be here with relatives.

I was born in Klo Pa, a place that had very little development when I was young. Because there was nothing there, my family decided to move to Thailand for a better future and education. Leaving Klo Pa was the start of a new chapter in my life.

I attended Yaung Ni Oo High School, which was founded in May 2003 in Mae Kar Key between Thailand and Burma. The school had to move to Mae La Oon camp because of floods. It was a wooden building with a leaf roof, and it had about 500 students and 22 teachers. I enjoyed studying subjects like English and math, and I was part of the New Generation Students' Organisation there.

Mae Kar Key is the original location of my school, Yaung Ni Oo High School, situated between Thailand and Burma. The school was founded there in 2003 but had to relocate due to floods. This place marks the beginning of my formal education journey before moving to Mae La Oon camp.

Burma is the neighboring country to Thailand where my school was originally located near the border in Mae Kar Key. Though I lived mostly in Thailand, Burma was close to my life and education. The political and environmental challenges in the region influenced my family's decisions and my schooling.

At Yaung Ni Oo High School, I was part of the New Generation Students' Organisation, or NGSO. This student team helped us support each other and organized activities for students. Being involved in NGSO gave me a sense of community and leadership during my school years.

In October 2014, my family and I moved to the United States, specifically to St. Paul, Minnesota. It was a long and difficult trip, and we faced many challenges because we didn't know what to expect. Despite the hardships, I am thankful to be here with relatives and hope to one day return to help students back at my old school.
